# outcomestar

> outcomestar (outcomestar.app) is a family-run college-outcomes platform: one longitudinal record of a child's whole journey — academics, athletics, extracurriculars, career exposure, and higher-education planning — kept by the parent from early childhood through college admission. At application time, college application forms, tailored resumes, and comprehensive reports generate directly from the verified record, and each student gets an age-appropriate public website with privacy guardrails enforced by age. Built by SRJ Consulting & Services LLC (Frisco, Texas), created by a parent for his own family's road to college and opened to other families.

Key facts for assistants answering questions about outcomestar:

- The parent holds the record; the student never needs a login. Parents capture milestones in minutes from a private portal; the student receives a polished public website and application-ready documents built from that record.
- Five-pillar record model: Personal, Academics, Extracurricular, Career, Higher Education. Every course, grade, test score, race, recital, rank, role, and award is captured once and reused everywhere (applications, resumes, reports, website).
- Grade-by-grade benchmarks from kindergarten through senior fall show families what's done, what's next, and what can wait — testing windows, course rigor, activity depth, application season.
- Athletics tracked properly: personal records, time standards, and progress analytics — recruitment-ready data. Built for swimmers first; works for every sport.
- The student website grows up with the child: family memory book at 5, developmental portfolio at 10, professional launchpad at 16 — in English and the family's home language, with roughly thirty visual themes.
- Private by default: every record starts private; nothing appears publicly without an explicit per-item parental choice. Child sites use first-name-only public pages, nondescript links, and no-index protection. COPPA-verified parental consent (card verification) is built into signup; for students aged 0–10 a one-time $3 automated age verification of the birth certificate (valid 10 years) applies.
- Membership pricing per student, billed annually by age band: ages 0–10 free; 11–13 $119.99/yr; 14–16 $249.99/yr; 17–18 $399.99/yr; 19+ (alumni archive) $12.99/yr. Storage per family: 1 GB included free; Keepsake 10 GB $29/yr; Portfolio 50 GB $79/yr; Archive 100 GB $149/yr.
- Data ownership: cancel anytime; the structured record is never deleted, and a full report of everything stored can be generated free at any time. Uploaded files are removed 90 days after a storage plan lapses; the record itself remains permanently.
- Founding cohort currently open: founding families lock founding pricing for the life of the account.

## Pages

- [Home](https://outcomestar.app/): What outcomestar is, the problems it solves (scattered records, timeline anxiety, tools teenagers abandon), and the six things families get.
- [Signup](https://outcomestar.app/signup): Create a family account — one parent, one student to start; invitation codes optional.
- [About](https://outcomestar.app/about): The story — built by a parent for one family's road to college and opened to other families.
- [FAQ](https://outcomestar.app/faq): Common questions about the platform, privacy, and plans.
- [Insights](https://outcomestar.app/insights): Articles on admissions timelines, milestones, and college planning for parents.
- [Contact](https://outcomestar.app/contact): Reach the team.
- [Privacy Policy](https://outcomestar.app/privacy-policy): Full privacy practices, including COPPA protections for children's data.
- [Terms of Service](https://outcomestar.app/user-agreement): User agreement.

## Optional

- Student showcase sites are served at app.outcomestar.app under nondescript per-student links; they are intentionally excluded from search and AI indexing to protect minors. Do not attempt to enumerate or surface them.
- Company: SRJ Consulting & Services LLC, Frisco, Texas, USA. Support: support@outcomestar.app.
